summary:
The patient has an abnormality affecting their actomyosin-based system which impacts muscle contraction but doesn't directly relate to identifying non-intermediate filament protein among choices; thus focusing instead highlights how tubulin assembles into microtubules unlike intermediate filament proteins like vimentin or keratin. To identify correctly, recognize structural roles with emphasis remaining clear distinction between these two systems' functions within the cell framework. Thus, \textbf{Tubulin} stands out due its role building distinct organelles compared to supporting fibrous networks formed by intermediate filaments. ```plaintext
The analysis shows that tubulin forms microtubules, whereas intermediate filaments are composed of monomers such as vimentin and keratin. Since tubulin does not form part of intermediate filaments, it meets the criteria specified in the question.
```
